item.title: R to @DeItaone: Source: https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/donald-trump/iran-negotiate-ceasefire-deal-trump-kharg-hormuz-oil-rcna263474
item.description: Source: nbcnews.com/politics/donald-…

item.title:
HORMUZ OIL FLOWS NEAR STANDSTILL, GOLDMAN SACHS SAYS
Goldman Sachs says oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz have nearly stopped.
Based on vessel tracking, flows have fallen from about 19.5 million barrels per day to just 0.5 million barrels per day over the past few days. After accounting for pipelines redirecting some supply, the net disruption to Persian Gulf exports is estimated at about 17.2 million barrels per day.
Tracking is difficult, but two independent vessel-count sources showed no oil tankers crossing the strait on March 12. Reuters reported that Iran did allow two India-flagged LPG carriers to pass, with one escorted by the Indian Navy.
Goldman says flows could recover through three scenarios: Iran selectively allowing some tankers through (such as those linked to China or India), a broader de-escalation of the conflict, or stronger naval protection for shipping.
However, the bank warns recovery could take longer than expected. So far, none of the reported tanker attacks have involved Asian-flagged ships, which is significant since Asian buyers account for the majority of Hormuz oil shipments.
item.description:
HORMUZ OIL FLOWS NEAR STANDSTILL, GOLDMAN SACHS SAYS

Goldman Sachs says oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz have nearly stopped.

Based on vessel tracking, flows have fallen from about 19.5 million barrels per day to just 0.5 million barrels per day over the past few days. After accounting for pipelines redirecting some supply, the net disruption to Persian Gulf exports is estimated at about 17.2 million barrels per day.

Tracking is difficult, but two independent vessel-count sources showed no oil tankers crossing the strait on March 12. Reuters reported that Iran did allow two India-flagged LPG carriers to pass, with one escorted by the Indian Navy.

Goldman says flows could recover through three scenarios: Iran selectively allowing some tankers through (such as those linked to China or India), a broader de-escalation of the conflict, or stronger naval protection for shipping.

However, the bank warns recovery could take longer than expected. So far, none of the reported tanker attacks have involved Asian-flagged ships, which is significant since Asian buyers account for the majority of Hormuz oil shipments.

item.title:
TRUMP: IRAN READY FOR CEASEFIRE — BUT “TERMS NOT GOOD ENOUGH”
President Donald Trump said Iran is willing to negotiate a ceasefire but he refuses to make a deal yet, saying the terms are “not good enough.”
In a phone interview with NBC News, Trump said any agreement would likely require Iran to completely abandon nuclear ambitions, though he declined to reveal specific conditions.
He also said the U.S. is working with other countries to secure the Strait of Hormuz as oil prices rise, and dismissed concerns about higher gas prices in the U.S.
Trump claimed recent U.S. strikes have destroyed much of Iran’s missile and drone capabilities and said Kharg Island was “totally demolished,” though energy infrastructure was spared.
He also questioned whether Iran’s new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is even alive, calling reports of his death a rumor.
Separately, Trump criticized Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, saying he is harder to negotiate with than Russian President Vladimir Putin as the U.S. tries to end the war in Ukraine.
item.description:
TRUMP: IRAN READY FOR CEASEFIRE — BUT “TERMS NOT GOOD ENOUGH”

President Donald Trump said Iran is willing to negotiate a ceasefire but he refuses to make a deal yet, saying the terms are “not good enough.”

In a phone interview with NBC News, Trump said any agreement would likely require Iran to completely abandon nuclear ambitions, though he declined to reveal specific conditions.

He also said the U.S. is working with other countries to secure the Strait of Hormuz as oil prices rise, and dismissed concerns about higher gas prices in the U.S.

Trump claimed recent U.S. strikes have destroyed much of Iran’s missile and drone capabilities and said Kharg Island was “totally demolished,” though energy infrastructure was spared.

He also questioned whether Iran’s new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is even alive, calling reports of his death a rumor.

Separately, Trump criticized Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, saying he is harder to negotiate with than Russian President Vladimir Putin as the U.S. tries to end the war in Ukraine.

item.title:
IRAN’S MIDDLE EAST AMBITIONS ARE DEAD
“Iran planned to dominate the Middle East and destroy Israel. Like Iran itself, those plans are now dead.” — President Donald J. Trump
item.description:
IRAN’S MIDDLE EAST AMBITIONS ARE DEAD

“Iran planned to dominate the Middle East and destroy Israel. Like Iran itself, those plans are now dead.”
— President Donald J. Trump

item.title: *TRUMP: US 'OBLITERATED' MILITARY TARGETS ON IRAN'S KHARG ISLAND
item.description: *TRUMP: US 'OBLITERATED' MILITARY TARGETS ON IRAN'S KHARG ISLAND

item.title:
U.S. STRIKE ON KHARG ISLAND — WARNING TO IRAN
Moments ago, on my orders, U.S. Central Command carried out one of the most powerful airstrikes in Middle East history, destroying every military target on Iran’s Kharg Island.
Our weapons are unmatched, but I chose not to strike the island’s oil infrastructure out of restraint. If Iran—or anyone—threatens free passage through the Strait of Hormuz, that decision will change immediately.
I rebuilt the U.S. military into the most powerful force on Earth. Iran cannot defend against anything we choose to strike.
Iran will never obtain a nuclear weapon or threaten the United States, the Middle East, or the world. Its military and those supporting this regime would be wise to stand down and preserve what remains of their country.
— President Donald J. Trump
item.description:
U.S. STRIKE ON KHARG ISLAND — WARNING TO IRAN

Moments ago, on my orders, U.S. Central Command carried out one of the most powerful airstrikes in Middle East history, destroying every military target on Iran’s Kharg Island.

Our weapons are unmatched, but I chose not to strike the island’s oil infrastructure out of restraint. If Iran—or anyone—threatens free passage through the Strait of Hormuz, that decision will change immediately.

I rebuilt the U.S. military into the most powerful force on Earth. Iran cannot defend against anything we choose to strike.

Iran will never obtain a nuclear weapon or threaten the United States, the Middle East, or the world. Its military and those supporting this regime would be wise to stand down and preserve what remains of their country.

— President Donald J. Trump

item.title: TRUMP: HOWEVER, SHOULD IRAN, OR ANYONE ELSE, DO ANYTHING TO INTERFERE WITH FREE AND SAFE PASSAGE OF SHIPS THROUGH STRAIT OF HORMUZ, I WILL IMMEDIATELY RECONSIDER THIS DECISION

item.title:
US OFFERS $10M BOUNTY FOR INFORMATION ON IRANIAN LEADERS
The U.S. Department of State is offering up to $10 million for information on senior officials tied to Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, according to the Rewards for Justice.
Those sought include Mojtaba Khamenei, Ali Asghar Hejazi, and Esmail Khatib, along with other senior defense and IRGC figures.
item.description:
US OFFERS $10M BOUNTY FOR INFORMATION ON IRANIAN LEADERS

The U.S. Department of State is offering up to $10 million for information on senior officials tied to Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, according to the Rewards for Justice.

Those sought include Mojtaba Khamenei, Ali Asghar Hejazi, and Esmail Khatib, along with other senior defense and IRGC figures.

item.description: ERDOGAN: TURKEY WILL NOT GET DRAGGED INTO IRAN WAR, BUT IS PREPARED AGAINST ALL THREATS
